title: Airehai
content:
Airehai, also known as Yitoikia II, is the second planet within the Yitoikia system. It is a warm water world within the Hydian Sector. Airehai supports a wide range of complex life, and is the homeworld of the Ichizhou species. Considerably more massive than worlds such as Equus or Terra Astra , Airehai's gravity is approximately 1.9 times that of Earth's. Although it has no natural satellites, numerous artificial satellites have been put into orbit by its inhabitants.

Astronomical Information

Airehai orbits its star, Yitoikia, at a distance of roughly 140 million kilometers (~0.93 AU). Airehai is the largest terrestrial planet in it's system, and the only one to support a robust atmosphere.

Climate and Geography

Airehai is a class-A terrestrial planet. Due to it's relative proximity to it's parent star, Airehai is largely warm and tropical in climate, with an average global surface temperature of 66 °F (18.9 °C). Due to large amount of surface water moderating the climate, Airehai experiences very little seasonal temperature variations. The planet experiences frequent tropical storms due to the high moisture content of the atmosphere.

Close to 90% of the planet's surface is covered in water, including small polar ice caps. Airehai's land is mostly concentrated in the small continent of Xirokabe in the northern hemisphere, while smaller, scattered islands make up the rest of the planets landmass.

Flora and Fauna

Airehai is home to a wide range of carbon-based life, from simple prokaryotic microorganism to large plant life to an intelligent humanoid species, the amphibious Ichizhou. Scientific consensus among the Ichizhou has long held that life on Airehai orginated around thermal vents at the bottom of the ocean, and mostly evolved in an aquatic environment. Terrestial life on Airehai only arose relatively late in the planet's biological history.

Having originated underwater — where light from Yitoikia was filtered — Airehai's plant life evolved to primarily use xanthophyll pigments to gather energy, giving them their distinctive golden yellow color. Most striking of Airehai's flora are the planet's mineralizing and budding "Calciblooms" - similar in some regards to old Earth's coral colonies. Often growing tens of meters tall, various species of calcifying plants thrive both throughout Airehai's seas and on land. Terrestrial calciblooms tend to be shorter than their marine counterparts, and thrive in mineral rich environments: coasts, salt flats, and in geologically active areas.

Most of the Airehai's animal life is aquatic; coming in almost every size, shape, and color imaginable. Perhaps the most famous non-sapient life from Airehai are the Kyodaissos: immense cetacean-like creatures that have been recorded as growing in excess of 100 meters in length. The Kyodaissos are also a cultural symbol to the Ichizhou, most notably in influencing the shape and design of Ichizhou spacecraft for centuries.

Ichizhou Civilization
